title: stack-graph — business model
note: Sample seed, modelled on bmc-schema / vpc-schema. The strategy-curator skill is the eventual write path; this is the read surface.

key_partnerships.text: Anthropic — Claude models + Claude Code primitives
key_partnerships.detail: The whole system rides Claude's native primitives (skills, agents, plugins, references). Structural: stack-graph has no value without the model + harness it instruments.
The portal deploys as static assets behind Cloudflare Access (Zero-Trust, fail-closed). Proven for be-civic's workspace; the same pattern carries here.

key_activities.text: Author + maintain the graph of .claude primitives
key_activities.detail: Keep the node/edge graph accurate, validated, and indexed as the operating environment evolves.
build/vendor.ts projects the graph into an installable plugin; the renderer projects it into the comprehension portal.

key_resources.detail: The single source of truth. Everything (plugin, portal, analytics) is a projection of it.
Install-free TS renderer (forked from be-civic's bc-renderer-core) that turns the graph + ledger into the deployed surfaces.

value_propositions.text: Model an agent operating environment as a reviewable graph, improved via a PR-gated loop
value_propositions.detail: The core bet: making the environment legible and reviewable beats ad-hoc .claude setups. Demonstrated on stack-graph itself; not yet proven with an external product.

value_propositions.text: Instantiate a product harness in hours, not weeks
value_propositions.detail: The harness-init + bindings-contract path is meant to stand up a new product workspace fast. Unvalidated — the be-civic Phase-C exercise is the first real test.

customer_relationships.detail: Hypothesis: an operator can adopt stack-graph from the plugin + handbook alone. No external operator has done this yet.
The loop is PR-gated — the operator approves at gates. Proven as the working mode for building stack-graph itself.

channels.detail: Intended distribution channel. Not yet published / measured.
The comprehension + adoption surface; it exists and renders, pending the live deploy.

customer_segments.detail: The primary segment — people who run a non-trivial .claude environment and feel the drift/legibility pain. stack-graph dogfoods this user.

customer_segments.text: Product teams running on Claude Code
customer_segments.detail: A broader segment if the harness model generalises. Unvalidated beyond be-civic.

cost_structure.detail: The dominant variable cost — multi-pass review + build runs. Scales with how much work flows through the loop.
Small fixed cost; static assets behind Access. Negligible relative to inference.

revenue_streams.text: Pre-revenue — viability deferred until a product proves demand
revenue_streams.detail: No revenue model committed. Viability is the explicit open risk; the factory is built first.

vpc.segment: Operators building an agent operating environment

customer_jobs.text: Stand up a reliable, reviewable agent environment
customer_jobs.detail: The functional job: go from ad-hoc primitives to a system you can trust and reason about.

customer_jobs.text: Know which primitives still earn their place
customer_jobs.detail: An ongoing job — prune what no longer pays its way. Depends on analytics that are input-gated until the loop runs.

pains.text: Ad-hoc .claude setups drift and break silently
pains.detail: Primitives accrete, conventions diverge, and nothing flags the drift until something breaks.

pains.text: No way to see the whole system at once
pains.detail: Before the graph browser, there was no single view of nodes + edges + health. It's why the graph surface exists.

gains.text: One comprehension surface over the whole environment
gains.detail: The portal unifies handbook + dashboard + graph + canvas + analytics into one navigable space.
The desired gain: insight turns into reviewed change. The loop exists; its end-to-end value is unproven at scale.

products_services.detail: What the operator engages with: the canonical graph, the rendered portal, and the installable plugin.

pain_relievers.text: The graph browser makes the whole system legible
pain_relievers.detail: The full graph with type-coloured nodes, typed edges, and health.
addresses: No way to see the whole system at once

pain_relievers.detail: Flags where traversals diverge from the authored graph. Input-gated until events exist.
addresses: Ad-hoc .claude setups drift and break silently

gain_creators.text: The dev-sprint loop turns insight into PRs
gain_creators.detail: The backbone is built; the full loop value is not yet evidenced.

problem_solution.claim: The pain relievers (legibility, drift-catching) address the most important pains (no overview, silent drift).

product_market.claim: Fit confirmed by evidence of demand from a paying/committed product. Not yet established — no commercial product.
